name: Get Pending Alert
summary: Retrieves the list of pending (active) price alerts for the logged-in user via a POST call. Each returned alert contains its type, id, trading symbol, exchange, token, remarks, validity, and the data value compared against the LTP.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: d
response_fields.description: Data to be compared with LTP

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes: Per the doc table, the success response is a JSON ARRAY (list) of alert objects, while the failure response is a single object. The curl example uses a placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/GetPendingAlert' — the real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI. The request body is x-www-form-urlencoded style: jData (the JSON object) and jKey joined with '&'. The success-response JSON is reproduced verbatim with the source's defects: smart/curly quotes (e.g. “ai_t”), the first key is "Stat" (capital S) although the field table documents it as "stat", and the "exch":"NSE" line is missing its trailing comma. Response field 'ai_t' is the alert type; 'd' is the data/price value compared against LTP.
issues: The cleaned-text success-response JSON contains smart quotes and defects (key "Stat" capital S vs documented "stat"; missing comma after "exch":"NSE") — reproduced verbatim as in the source. The jData object documents only 'uid'; no other request body fields are specified. The success-response 'stat' example shows lowercase "ok" while the failure response shows "Not_Ok".
